data.text: The full CLI test run is in flight. I’m waiting on the first compiler pass so I can patch the remaining legacy callers in one batch instead of piecemeal.

The compiler found the next seam: a few agent-facing interface types still advertise the old variadic methods, so `Store` no longer satisfies them. I’m updating those interfaces and the failing tests together so the repo converges on one signature instead of papering over each callsite.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go && printf '\\n---AC-GATE---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' cli/internal/agent/ac_quality.go && printf '\\n---TRY ATTEMPT TEST---\\n' && sed -n '360,430p' cli/internal/agent/try/attempt_test.go"

data.text: The remaining breakage is concentrated in the agent package’s narrow store interfaces. I’m updating those interfaces to the new typed methods now, which should fix both the `Store` conformance errors and the tests that wrap it through those interfaces.

data.text: I’ve now got a concrete hit list from the compiler and scan. The remaining work is repetitive but bounded: replace the old direct calls in tests and a few helper interfaces, then rerun the suite until the compiler is clean.

I hit a path typo while batching the mechanical replacements, so I’m switching to relative paths for the rest of the edit pass. That keeps the patching precise and avoids wasting time on the long execution-worktree prefix.

package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// initRepoWithBead creates a fresh git repo with one committed file, an
// initialized .ddx bead store containing one bead, and (optionally) writes
// the given check YAML under .ddx/checks/.
func initRepoWithBead(t *testing.T, beadID string, labels []string, checkYAML string) string {
	t.Helper()
	root := t.TempDir()

	gitInit := func(args ...string) {
		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", root}, args...)...)
		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, out)
	}
	gitInit("init", "-q", "-b", "main")
	gitInit("config", "user.email", "t@example.com")
	gitInit("config", "user.name", "Test")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
	gitInit("add", ".")
	gitInit("commit", "-q", "-m", "init")

	ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
	store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: beadID, Title: "ac test", Labels: labels}))

	if checkYAML != "" {
		checksDir := filepath.Join(ddxDir, "checks")
		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(checksDir, 0o755))
		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(checksDir, "fixture.yaml"), []byte(checkYAML), 0o644))
	}

	return root
}

func TestAcRun_PassExitsZero(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-pass"
	yaml := `
name: passer
command: 'printf ''{"status":"pass","message":"ok"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[PASS] passer")
}

func TestAcRun_BlockExitsNonZero(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-block"
	yaml := `
name: blocker
command: 'printf ''{"status":"block","message":"nope"}'' > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/$CHECK_NAME.json"'
when: pre_merge
`
	root := initRepoWithBead(t, beadID, nil, yaml)

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(root).NewRootCommand(), "ac", "run", beadID)
	require.Error(t, err)
	require.Contains(t, out, "[BLOCK] blocker")
}

func TestAcRun_ErrorOnMissingResultFile(t *testing.T) {
	beadID := "ddx-ac-err"
	yaml := `

---BEAD HUNAN UPDATE---
	}
	nh := &bead.Bead{
		ID:       "ddx-needs-human-json",
		Title:    "Needs operator",
		Priority: 0,
		Status:   bead.StatusProposed,
		Labels:   []string{"area:cli"},
	}
	plain := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-plain-json", Title: "Plain"}
	_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, nh, plain)
	require.NoError(t, store.Update(nh.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
		bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, meta)
	}))

	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "needs-human", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var rows []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &rows))
	require.Len(t, rows, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, nh.ID, rows[0]["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), rows[0]["priority"])
	assert.Equal(t, nh.Title, rows[0]["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Reason, rows[0]["reason"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Since, rows[0]["since"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Source, rows[0]["source"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.SuggestedAction, rows[0]["suggested_action"])
	assert.Equal(t, meta.Summary, rows[0]["summary"])
	assert.NotContains(t, rows[0]["labels"], bead.LabelNeedsHuman)
}

func TestBeadNeedsHumanCommand_Text(t *testing.T) {
	nh := &bead.Bead{
		ID:       "ddx-needs-human-text",
		Title:    "Needs text output",
		Priority: 1,
		Status:   bead.StatusProposed,
	}
	_, factory, store := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, nh)
	require.NoError(t, store.Update(nh.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
		bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{Reason: "operator decision required"})
	}))

	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "needs-human")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, nh.ID)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "P1")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "Needs text output")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "operator decision required")
}

func TestBeadReadyExcludesProposedByDefault(t *testing.T) {
	ready := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-ready-normal", Title: "Normal ready"}
	proposed := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-ready-human", Title: "Human ready", Status: bead.StatusProposed}
	_, factory, _ := setupBeadHumanEnv(t, ready, proposed)


---BEAD RECONCILE---
		Title:  "stale",
		Labels: []string{bead.LabelNoChangesUnjustified},
		Extra: map[string]any{
			bead.ExtraLastStatus: "no_changes",
			"events": []any{
				map[string]any{"kind": "execute-bead", "summary": "success", "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
			},
		},
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), b))

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "reconcile", "--dry-run")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "would repair")
	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, got.Extra, bead.ExtraLastStatus)
}

func TestBeadReconcileCommandApplyMutatesThroughStore(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ddxroot.DirName))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init(context.Background()))
	b := &bead.Bead{
		ID:     "ddx-stale",
		Title:  "stale",
		Labels: []string{bead.LabelNoChangesUnjustified},
		Extra: map[string]any{
			bead.ExtraLastStatus: "no_changes",
			"events": []any{
				map[string]any{"kind": "execute-bead", "summary": "success", "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"},
			},
		},
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), b))

	out, err := executeCommand(NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand(), "bead", "reconcile", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "repaired")
	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

data.text: The remaining failures are now mostly mechanical receiver-method migrations. I’m applying a scoped scripted replacement across the bead-related CLI/server files for `store.Create`/`Update`/`Close` callsites so I can get back to compiler-driven cleanup instead of hand-editing dozens of identical lines.

input.command:
/bin/zsh -lc "python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
roots = [Path('cli/cmd'), Path('cli/internal/agent'), Path('cli/internal/server'), Path('cli/internal/server/graphql'), Path('cli/internal/attemptmetrics')]
repls = [
    (re.compile(r'\\bstore\\.Create\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'store.Create(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\bstore\\.Update\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'store.Update(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\bstore\\.Close\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'store.Close(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\bbaseStore\\.Create\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'baseStore.Create(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\bbaseStore\\.Update\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'baseStore.Update(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\br\\.store\\.Create\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'r.store.Create(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\br\\.store\\.Update\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'r.store.Update(context.Background(), '),
    (re.compile(r'\\br\\.store\\.Close\\((?"'!context'"\\.Background\\()'), 'r.store.Close(context.Background(), '),
]
for root in roots:
    for path in root.rglob('*.go'):
        text = path.read_text()
        orig = text
        for pat, repl in repls:
            text = pat.sub(repl, text)
        if text "'!= orig:
            path.write_text(text)
PY'

data.text: The scripted pass was the right move, but it also means I need to sanity-check for any accidental double-wrapping of request contexts. I’m scanning for `context.Background(), r.Context()`-style damage before I trust the build again.

No accidental double-wrapping showed up in the scan, so the mechanical pass stayed clean. I’ve kicked off a narrower compile/test run on the affected packages now to flush out whatever the compiler still sees.

I found the bug in the bulk pass: I ran it from `cli/`, so the paths were off by one `cli/` segment and nothing meaningful changed. I’m rerunning the same replacement against the correct roots now, then I’ll add the couple of missing `context` imports the compile just exposed.

1165401       39:51  6.0  0.0 /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/codex exec --json --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox -C /Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-cc298105-20260606T161500-c4ea0d73 -m gpt-5.4-mini <execute-bead>   <instructions> You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <parent-id> <child-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-cc298105">     <title>bead store: migrate read APIs to ctx-first signatures</title>     <description> PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational bead Store read surface to be typed and ctx-first, but the read methods still accept untyped variadic arguments. This keeps the local bead package API ambiguous and makes the ddx-464feba2 caller sweep too large to execute as one bead.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:80-84 declares BeadReader with ReadAll(args ...any), ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), and Get(args ...any).
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:181-240 exposes Store.ReadAll(args ...any) and Store.ReadAllFiltered(args ...any), using storeCallContext from cli/internal/bead/store.go:134-141.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:599-625 exposes Store.Get(args ...any), using argument scanning for the bead ID.
- cli/internal/bead/archive.go:158-174 exposes Store.GetWithArchive(id string) and currently supplies context.Background() internally instead of accepting caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the read-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: ReadAll(ctx context.Context), ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool), Get(ctx context.Context, id string), and GetWithArchive(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadReader in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only read-method callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests that prove canceled contexts are honored and GetWithArchive forwards the supplied context instead of creating a background context.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <parent-id> <child-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-38801c65">     <title>bead store: migrate mutation APIs to ctx-first signatures</title>     <description> PROBLEM
TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
- TD-027.

PARENT/DEPS
- Parent/split source: ddx-464feba2.
- Deps: no additional deps; this child can land independently of the setup/id and read signature children.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. cli/internal/bead/store.go exposes Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error, Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error, and Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error with typed parameters and no variadic argument parsing for those methods.
2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadLifecycle.Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error while preserving Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error.
3. All Go callsites for Store.Create, Store.Update, and Store.Close pass an explicit context and typed arguments.
4. Add or update deterministic tests named TestStoreCreate_RejectsCanceledContext, TestStoreUpdate_RejectsCanceledContext, and TestStoreClose_RejectsCanceledContext.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestStore(Create|Update|Close)_RejectsCanceledContext' passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>phase:iterate, area:bead, area:storage, kind:refactor, spec:TD-027</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-3fd19af1" base-rev="b74eda8be0079a9e9f2eb7dc124bf3fe5f276a9e" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260606T161700-a47fd8fb"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. - Mutation APIs: Create, Update, and Close.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.

TD-027 requires the foundational Store mutation surface to accept context explicitly, but Create, Update, and Close still accept untyped variadic arguments. This hides cancellation behavior and forces the original ddx-464feba2 bead to mix Store API changes with a repo-wide caller migration.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/bead/backend.go:86-89 declares BeadLifecycle with Create(args ...any), while Apply already uses ctx-first shape.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:517-596 exposes Store.Create(args ...any), scans for *Bead, and calls GenID through the variadic path.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:627-655 exposes Store.Update(args ...any), scans for id and mutate function.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1499-1535 exposes Store.Close(args ...any), scans for id and then calls lifecycle helpers without explicit caller context.

PROPOSED FIX
Change only the mutation-side Store methods to typed ctx-first signatures: Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead), Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)), and Close(ctx context.Context, id string). Update BeadLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. Migrate only Create, Update, and Close callsites across the repo to pass context explicitly. Add deterministic bead package tests for canceled-context behavior and typed argument validation.

NON-SCOPE
- Init and GenID signature migration.
- Read APIs: ReadAll, ReadAllFiltered, Get, and GetWithArchive.
- Event method ctx threading: AppendEvent, Events, and EventsByKind.
- Removing workflow helper methods beyond the foundational signatures this bead changes.
- Caller migration unrelated to Create, Update, and Close.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT
